item.title: Antimicrobial resistance in foodborne bacteria remains a public health concern in Europe
item.description: Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) in common foodborne bacteria such as Salmonella and Campylobacter continues to be a public health concern across Europe...

item.title: Brainwaves of mothers and children synchronize when playing together—even in an acquired language
item.description: Interbrain synchrony is the simultaneous activity of neural networks across the brains of people who are socially interacting—for example, talking, le...

item.title: Home care: The Dutch model that challenges bureaucracy
item.description: Bureaucracy once swallowed Dutch home care. Buurtzorg flipped the script by trusting nurses and focusing on purpose.

item.title: C-reactive protein links to ventricular repolarization in coronary artery disease, study finds
item.description: A new study reveals that the predictive power of key inflammatory markers, such as C-reactive protein, shifts fundamentally depending on whether a pat...

item.title: Allergic conjunctivitis linked to increased risk for keratoconus
item.description: For patients aged 5 to 25 years, allergic conjunctivitis is associated with an increased risk for keratoconus and increased corneal diagnostic procedu...
